Perfect access is a [[hydroplane]] caused by lack of [[theory of mind]]. It can cause a lot of problems for people.

It is a hydroplane often held by progressives on Twitter relating to the metaphysics of morals. It holds two principles: firstly that morals are universally clear and epistemically accessible to all people, with the implication the heretic knows exactly what is right and everyone else agrees with them, and secondly that people’s wills and ability to make moral decisions are never in question. The natural conclusion of this metaphysical system is that if you perceive someone as morally transgressing it means they are actively and knowingly morally transgressing. This leads to climates of extreme intolerance and stifled dialogue. The solution is to accept that even when moral questions have the right answers most people do not have the knowledge to access them and to use institutions such as the Church to establish moral consensus and guide people to the right path.<ref>https://evolution.faith/hydroplaning/</ref>
== References ==

[[Category:Hydroplanes]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Perfect Access]][[sn:Perfect Access]]
[[Category:Pages]]
